title: Course Schedule
problem_statement: Determine whether you can finish all courses given prerequisite pairs.
intuition: This is cycle detection in a directed graph; if a cycle exists, the schedule is impossible.

approaches.name: DFS cycle detection
approaches.description: Use DFS with recursion stack tracking to identify back edges that indicate cycles.
approaches.why_not: Kahn’s algorithm is also valid; DFS is simpler if only feasibility is required.

interview_qa.q: What do recursion states 1 and 2 represent?
interview_qa.a: 1 means visiting, 2 means fully processed; revisiting 1 indicates a cycle.

interview_qa.q: Why is this a graph problem?
interview_qa.a: Courses and prerequisites form a directed graph where edges impose dependency order.
